Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main wires Groq to ProviderIcon-groq, and that SVG's path data matches ProviderIcon-grok. I did not run the app or capture a live Settings screenshot in this read-only review.

target.title: fix: use Groq logo for Groq provider icon
target.body:
## Summary

The Settings page was showing the **Grok** (xAI) logo for the **Groq** provider because `ProviderIcon-groq.svg` was a byte-for-byte duplicate of `ProviderIcon-grok.svg`.

This PR replaces `ProviderIcon-groq.svg` with the actual Groq 'Q' wordmark so the two providers are visually distinct.

## Before / After

- Before: Settings → Groq row showed the xAI Grok glyph.
- After: Settings → Groq row shows the Groq Q glyph.

## Notes

- Only `Sources/CodexBar/Resources/ProviderIcon-groq.svg` is touched.
- Format/sizing (viewBox `0 0 24 24`, `width/height=100`, `fill=#FFFFFF`) matches the sibling `ProviderIcon-grok.svg` so theme/rendering behaviour is unchanged.
- No code, descriptor, or test changes are needed — `GroqProviderDescriptor` already references `iconResourceName: "ProviderIcon-groq"`.
target.author: kiankyars
